_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize federal-House batch: MO-06 (Levine, active), MT-01 (Cleveland LOST primary / Forstag WON nominee), NE-02 (Rhoades LOST / Askins LOST). All conventional progressives; conservative-rubric cells FALSE where documented contrary, null where no evidence. Candidacy-currency fixes are the headline: 3 lost their primaries, 1 won the nomination, 1 still in primary.

set.office: U.S. Representative MO-06 (2026 D primary candidate · Aug 4 2026 primary · Graves open seat)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified active candidate (FEC H6MO06278, filed 2025-05-29; 3-way D primary Aug 4 2026). Scored from campaign Key Issues page + FEC.

matthew-levine-mo-06.notes_set: Kansas City TSA officer / former accountant / substitute special-ed teacher. Conventional progressive: pro-choice ('women deserve the right to choose'), pro-LGBTQ ('not performative'), ICE-accountability/pro-immigrant, populist-left economics, public-school/free-meals education, gun-reform (background checks, age limits, 'common sense reforms' while rhetorically affirming 2A). FEC shows no PAC money (small-dollar + self-funded). No faith statement found. District R+19 (long-shot general). Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Pro-choice: 'Women deserve the right to choose what happens to their own body.'

1.note: Pro-choice; no abortion-abolition advocacy.

2.note: No statement on embryonic stem cell / IVF.

3.note: No statement on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: FEC report through 2026-03-31: no PAC funding at all (small-dollar individual + self-funded); no abortion-industry PAC money.

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ ('My support for the LGBTQ+ community is not performative. It is personal.'); does not affirm one-man-one-woman.

1.note: Pro-LGBTQ rights advocacy; no opposition to same-sex marriage.

2.note: Blanket LGBTQ-affirming stance ('fighting for your rights, freedom, dignity, humanity').

3.note: No statement on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Affirms / promotes LGBTQ rights in public policy.

0.note: Public-school oriented (free meals, tutoring); no explicit school-choice support or opposition.

1.note: No statement on parental consent for minors' care.
No statement on conscience exemptions.
No statement on sound money / audit the Fed.

2.note: No statement on a balanced-budget amendment.

3.note: No statement on usury / tax structure specifics.

1.note: No statement on voter ID.

4.note: No statement on private election funding.

0.note: No statement on a border barrier.

1.note: Enforcement-restraint stance: 'I do not believe that ICE should be allowed to run rampant'; demands warrants — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

0.note: Backs background checks, age restrictions, 'common sense reforms' — contrary to permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports expanded background checks, safe-storage, age limits, 'common sense reforms.'

2.note: Pro-gun-reform; no support for repealing NFA/GCA.

1.note: No statement on forever wars / AUMFs.

3.note: FEC: no PAC money at all; no foreign-linked PAC or AIPAC money.

0.note: No statement on pharma mandates / informed consent.

1.note: No statement on pharma liability shields.

2.note: No statement on Big Ag consolidation.

3.note: Generic 'support farmers' only; no small-farm/raw-milk specifics.

set.office: U.S. Representative MT-01 (2026 D primary candidate — LOST 2026-06-02 primary, 3rd ~22%; Zinke open seat)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified: LOST the 2026-06-02 MT-01 Democratic primary (3rd of 4, ~22%); Sam Forstag won the nomination. Zinke retired (open seat). Scored from campaign policy page before exit.

russ-cleveland.notes_set: Navy veteran / cattle rancher (St. Regis) / former banker / childcare entrepreneur. Self-branded 'independent / dirt-road Democrat,' rejected ALL PAC and corporate money (individual donations only). Progressive policy: pass WHPA (pro-choice), Equality Act ('LGBTQIA2S+ rights are human rights'), Medicare for All, end ICE funding/path to citizenship, Israel arms embargo + cut DoD spending, antitrust enforcement, USDA grant streamlining for ranchers. Rhetorically pro-2A ('right to own a firearm') but qualified by 'secure storage and accountability.' No faith statement found. LOST 2026-06-02 primary (3rd, ~22%) — out of the race. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Supports Women's Health Protection Act codifying broad abortion access.

1.note: Pro-choice (WHPA); no abortion-abolition advocacy.

2.note: No statement on embryonic stem cell / IVF.

4.note: Rejected ALL PAC/corporate money (individual donations only) — no abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: 'LGBTQIA2S+ rights are human rights'; supports Equality Act — does not affirm one-man-one-woman.

4.note: Affirms/promotes LGBTQ rights in policy (Equality Act).

0.note: Proposes universal childcare/pre-K & education restructuring; no explicit school-choice stance.

2.note: No statement on a balanced-budget amendment.

4.note: Supports general antitrust/consumer-protection enforcement but no anti-WEF/ESG framing; insufficient for this cell.

1.note: No statement on voter ID.

4.note: Campaign-finance reform plank only; no Zuckerbucks position.

0.note: 'Secure borders' rhetoric but no border-wall position.

1.note: 'End ICE funding altogether'; remove red tape to citizenship — contrary to mandatory deportation.

Israel arms embargo + DoD cuts but 'support Ukraine's resistance'; no clean forever-war-withdrawal stance.

set.office: U.S. Representative MT-01 (2026 Democratic NOMINEE — won 2026-06-02 primary; Zinke open seat; faces Aaron Flint R)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified: WON the 2026-06-02 MT-01 Democratic primary (~37%), now the Democratic nominee vs. Aaron Flint (R). Zinke retired (open seat). Scored from campaign policies page + Vote411/MTFP.

sam-forstag.notes_set: Former smokejumper / wildland firefighter, union VP (NFFE-IAM Local 60), former legislative lobbyist (ACLU of Montana; Planned Parenthood Advocates of MT in 2023). Down-the-line progressive: codify Roe + expand family planning, repeal GOP tax cuts + millionaire minimum tax + $15 wage, ban Super PACs/overturn Citizens United, comprehensive immigration reform/pathway to citizenship, enforce War Powers Resolution / stop Iran war. Endorsed by Bernie Sanders, AOC, Congressional Progressive Caucus, MT AFL-CIO, trans/LGBTQ state legislators. No faith statement found. WON nomination 2026-06-02. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

set.office: U.S. Representative NE-02 (2026 D primary candidate — LOST 2026-05-12 primary, 3rd; Bacon retired / open seat)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified: LOST the 2026-05-12 NE-02 Democratic primary (3rd); Denise Powell won the nomination vs. Brinker Harding (R). Record corrected: Don Bacon RETIRED (announced 2025-06-30), seat is open — prior 'Bacon lost' note was wrong. Has a real official record (NE Public Service Commission 2015–2023). Scored from BallotReady/campaign + PSC record.

crystal-rhoades.notes_set: Lifelong Omahan; former Nebraska Public Service Commissioner (2015–2023) and current Douglas County District Court Clerk. Conventional progressive: pro-choice ('women must have the right to choose'), self-listed 'LGBTQ Advocacy,' public-school funding (no choice), path-to-citizenship immigration, public-option/Medicare-for-All, cannabis legalization, abolish the Electoral College. Official record: voted NO on Keystone XL route (2017) on landowner-due-process/aquifer grounds; pro-regulation rideshare-licensing stance. No faith statement found. LOST 2026-05-12 primary (3rd) — out of the race. Record also corrected: Bacon retired, not defeated. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

2.note: No statement on embryonic stem cell / IVF.

4.note: No documented refusal pledge and no abortion-industry funding found either way.

set.office: U.S. Representative NE-02 (2026 D primary candidate — LOST 2026-05-12 primary, 4th ~6%; Bacon retired / open seat)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified: LOST the 2026-05-12 NE-02 Democratic primary (4th of 6, ~6%); Denise Powell won the nomination vs. Brinker Harding (R). Record corrected: Don Bacon RETIRED (announced 2025-06-30), seat is open — prior 'Bacon lost' note was wrong. Thin policy record; scored only documented positions.

kishla-askins.notes_set: 30-year Navy veteran (corpsman / emergency-medicine PA-C), former Deputy Assistant Secretary at the VA (Biden-Harris admin). Gay woman, married to her wife since 2015; survived two DADT investigations; opposes the transgender military service ban ('a medical diagnosis for politicians not to weigh in on'). Emphasized congressional war-powers oversight on the 2026 Iran operation. 'Whole Health America' plan to break up healthcare consolidation. Endorsed by VoteVets PAC. No faith statement found; most issue areas returned no documented position. LOST 2026-05-12 primary (4th, ~6%) — out of the race. Record also corrected: Bacon retired, not defeated. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.
